stem: A ecology study surveys 25 sampled plots and observes 11 positive outcomes out of 25 (44.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (12 observations, rate = 33.3%) and Group B (13 observations, rate = 53.8%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 43% and 57% of the population. Estimate the true species occurrence rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.44. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.4422 (range [0.4288, 0.4557]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2667, 0.6293]. Point estimate: 0.4422.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.5: 0.5×0.346 + 0.5×0.5385 = 0.4422.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
